sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, clicking the center cell turned it green again. So now the 3x3 grid has 4 green cells: top-right, middle-left, center, bottom-right. This doesn't match the left panel pattern anymore.
Let me toggle the center back to gray to restore the correct pattern.
But wait, the level is still not completed. So just matching the pattern is not enough.
Let me think about what else could be needed. The bar has a cursor that can be moved. Maybe I need to move the cursor to a specific position to "confirm" the pattern.
Looking at the bar more carefully, the cursor is a 2x2 blue block. The bar's right section is 4 rows tall (rows 19-22) and 8 columns wide (cols 34-41). The cursor can move within this area.
Maybe the cursor's position corresponds to a "submit" button. For example, moving the cursor to the far left of the right section might trigger the level completion check.
Let me try moving the cursor to the far left of the right section and clicking.
Actually, let me first restore the correct pattern by toggling the center back to gray, and then try moving the cursor.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Restore correct pattern (toggle center back to gray) and then try moving cursor
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 30}])  # toggle center to gray
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
